summary: The extrait Kbis is the standard, up-to-date register extract for any company registered at the RCS, delivered by the greffe / Infogreffe. It evidences the registered particulars (SIREN, form, denomination, seat, object, capital, duration, managers/dirigeants and insolvency judgments). It does not list ordinary shareholders.

evidence.basis: Infogreffe – official document 'Extrait Kbis'
evidence.quote: L'extrait Kbis (...) constitue la véritable carte d'identité à jour d'une entreprise immatriculée au registre du commerce et des sociétés (RCS).
evidence.note: Standard extract ordered by SIREN or company name; delivered paper or electronic.

evidence.basis: Service-Public – Comment obtenir un extrait K ou Kbis (F21000)
evidence.note: Confirms Kbis is the official extract of the RCS registration for a company (personne morale).

caveats: The Kbis for a SARL identifies the manager(s)/dirigeants, NOT the ordinary associés and their shareholdings — do not treat it as proof of the shareholder structure.
One secondary site claims the Kbis lists 'autres associés ou membres'; this is imprecise for capital companies and not relied on — UNVERIFIED against a primary source.

documentLabel: Articles of association naming the members and the allocation of shares (statuts)
summary: France has no standalone filed shareholder list (no equivalent of the German §40 GmbHG Gesellschafterliste). For a SARL, however, the identity of the associés and the allocation of parts sociales must by law appear in the statuts, which are a filed act publicly obtainable via Infogreffe / Data INPI. Because transfers of parts require an amendment of the statuts, the current statuts on file are the registry document that evidences the current members.

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. L223-7, al. 4 (SARL)
evidence.quote: La répartition des parts sociales est mentionnée dans les statuts.
evidence.note: Mandatory content of SARL statuts; the parts are nominative and named to each associé.

evidence.basis: INPI – Data INPI / dépôt d'actes (statuts filed and publicly consultable)
evidence.quote: Les actes et statuts depuis 1993 (...) sont mis à disposition.
evidence.note: Statuts are a filed act, ordered separately from the Kbis, available on Data INPI / Infogreffe.

caveats: This is 'proof via the statuts', not via a dedicated shareholder-list document — the statuts must be separately ordered (not delivered with the Kbis).

Currency depends on filing discipline: a cession de parts requires amending and re-filing the statuts to be opposable to third parties, but there can be lag or non-compliance — INFERENCE / practical caveat.
The exact article governing opposabilité/registration of a SARL parts transfer was not verbatim-verified — UNVERIFIED.

summary: The statuts are the constitution and are a filed act at the greffe/RNE, publicly obtainable via Infogreffe or Data INPI. They are a separately-ordered document (not part of the Kbis).

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. L210-2
evidence.quote: La forme, la durée qui ne peut excéder quatre-vingt-dix-neuf ans, la dénomination sociale, le siège social, l'objet social et le montant du capital social sont déterminés par les statuts de la société.

evidence.basis: INPI – Data INPI (public consultation of acts and statuts since 1993)
evidence.quote: Les actes et statuts depuis 1993 (...) sont mis à disposition.

caveats: Ordered separately from the Kbis (dépôt d'actes).

summary: Same as for any registered company: the Kbis is the standard up-to-date RCS extract delivered by the greffe / Infogreffe. It shows the président and other declared dirigeants, not the actionnaires.

evidence.basis: Infogreffe – official document 'Extrait Kbis'
evidence.quote: L'extrait Kbis (...) constitue la véritable carte d'identité à jour d'une entreprise immatriculée au registre du commerce et des sociétés (RCS).

caveats: The Kbis does NOT identify the shareholders of a SAS.

summary: The registry does not evidence who the current shareholders of a SAS are. Actionnaires are recorded in the company's own private registre des mouvements de titres and comptes d'actionnaires, which are kept by the company, not filed at the RCS and not public. Share transfers do not amend the statuts, so the filed statuts do not track ownership either.

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. R228-9 (registre des mouvements de titres — kept by the company)
evidence.quote: En cas de transfert de titres, l'inscription est faite (...) et le registre mentionne les nom, prénoms et domicile du cédant et du cessionnaire.
evidence.note: This register is maintained by the issuing company, not by the RCS; it is not filed and not public.

evidence.basis: Registre des bénéficiaires effectifs (RBE) — Code monétaire et financier, art. L561-46
evidence.quote: Depuis le 31 juillet 2024, l'accès aux informations du RBE est restreint (...) réservé aux personnes justifiant d'un intérêt légitime.
evidence.note: Even the RBE only records beneficial owners (>25% or effective control), not the full cap table, and public access has been closed since the CJEU ruling of 22 Nov 2022 — so it is not a substitute shareholder list.

caveats: Founding shareholders may appear in the initial constitutive statuts, but that is NOT proof of the CURRENT shareholders and must not be treated as such.
The RBE is filed but (a) access-restricted since 31 July 2024 and (b) covers beneficial owners, not the legal shareholder list.

summary: The statuts are a filed act, publicly obtainable via Infogreffe / Data INPI, ordered separately from the Kbis.

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. L210-2
evidence.quote: La forme, la durée qui ne peut excéder quatre-vingt-dix-neuf ans, la dénomination sociale, le siège social, l'objet social et le montant du capital social sont déterminés par les statuts de la société.

evidence.basis: INPI – Data INPI
evidence.quote: Les actes et statuts depuis 1993 (...) sont mis à disposition.

caveats: Ordered separately from the Kbis; a SAS's statuts do not record the actionnaires (only founders, and share transfers do not amend them).

summary: Standard up-to-date RCS extract from the greffe / Infogreffe. It shows the declared corporate officers (administrateurs / directeurs généraux or directoire/conseil de surveillance), not the actionnaires.

evidence.basis: Infogreffe – official document 'Extrait Kbis'
evidence.quote: L'extrait Kbis (...) constitue la véritable carte d'identité à jour d'une entreprise immatriculée au registre du commerce et des sociétés (RCS).

caveats: The Kbis does NOT identify the shareholders of an SA.

summary: The registry does not evidence who the current shareholders of an SA are. Shares are recorded in the company's private registre des mouvements de titres and comptes d'actionnaires (or with an account-keeping intermediary for admitted securities); these are not filed at the RCS and not public. Transfers do not amend the statuts.

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. R228-9 (registre des mouvements de titres — kept by the company)
evidence.quote: le registre mentionne les nom, prénoms et domicile du cédant et du cessionnaire.
evidence.note: Register held by the issuer, not filed at the RCS, not public.

evidence.basis: Registre des bénéficiaires effectifs (RBE) — Code monétaire et financier, art. L561-46
evidence.quote: l'accès aux informations du RBE est restreint (...) aux personnes justifiant d'un intérêt légitime.
evidence.note: RBE records beneficial owners only, and public access has been closed since 31 July 2024 following CJEU 22 Nov 2022.

caveats: Founding shareholders in the initial statuts are not proof of the current shareholders.
RBE is filed but access-restricted and covers only beneficial owners, not the legal shareholder register.

summary: The statuts are a filed act, publicly obtainable via Infogreffe / Data INPI, ordered separately from the Kbis.

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. L210-2
evidence.quote: La forme, la durée qui ne peut excéder quatre-vingt-dix-neuf ans, la dénomination sociale, le siège social, l'objet social et le montant du capital social sont déterminés par les statuts de la société.

evidence.basis: INPI – Data INPI
evidence.quote: Les actes et statuts depuis 1993 (...) sont mis à disposition.

summary: Standard up-to-date RCS extract from the greffe / Infogreffe. For an SNC the extract also reveals the unlimited, joint-and-several liability of the partners via the legal form, and the associés en nom (who are traders/commerçants) are recorded in the RCS registration.

evidence.basis: Infogreffe – official document 'Extrait Kbis'
evidence.quote: L'extrait Kbis (...) constitue la véritable carte d'identité à jour d'une entreprise immatriculée au registre du commerce et des sociétés (RCS).

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. L221-1 (SNC — associés indéfiniment et solidairement responsables)
evidence.quote: Les associés en nom collectif ont tous la qualité de commerçant et répondent indéfiniment et solidairement des dettes sociales.

caveats: Whether every associé (not just gérants) is printed on the SNC Kbis was not verbatim-confirmed from a primary source; it follows from the RCS obligation to register indefinitely-liable partners — INFERENCE.

documentLabel: Register extract identifying the partners (Kbis), plus statuts for the share allocation
summary: Unlike capital companies, an SNC's partners are provable from the registry. The associés (all traders, indefinitely and jointly liable) are recorded at the RCS and appear on the Kbis, and the répartition des parts is stated in the statuts. Parts are non-negotiable and cannot be transferred without unanimous consent, and any change of partner requires an amendment of the statuts filed at the RCS — so the on-file record tracks the current partners.

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. L221-13 (parts sociales — cession requires unanimous consent)
evidence.quote: Les parts sociales ne peuvent être cédées qu'avec le consentement de tous les associés.
evidence.note: Any partner change is a statutory amendment, filed at the RCS.

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. L221-14 (opposabilité of a parts transfer)
evidence.quote: La cession n'est opposable aux tiers qu'après (...) dépôt (...) au registre du commerce et des sociétés.
evidence.note: Transfer becomes effective against third parties only after RCS filing.

caveats: The Kbis identifies WHO the partners are; the exact number of parts each holds comes from the statuts (répartition des parts) — a separately-ordered document.
Exact verbatim wording of L221-13/L221-14 taken from the SNC chapter section, not each isolated article page — minor UNVERIFIED risk on exact phrasing.

summary: The statuts are a filed act, publicly obtainable via Infogreffe / Data INPI, ordered separately from the Kbis. For an SNC they also carry the répartition des parts among the named partners.

evidence.basis: Code de commerce, art. L210-2
evidence.quote: La forme, la durée qui ne peut excéder quatre-vingt-dix-neuf ans, la dénomination sociale, le siège social, l'objet social et le montant du capital social sont déterminés par les statuts de la société.

evidence.basis: INPI – Data INPI
evidence.quote: Les actes et statuts depuis 1993 (...) sont mis à disposition.
